repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Only viable on low-mileage; often cheaper to replace the engine
Viable — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Replacement often £5,000–£8,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d? Check These First
buyIf: * 2021+ facelift with AJ20D6 Ingenium I6 (the more reliable engine)
* 2016–2020 with documented oil pump and crankshaft bearing history
* Full service history with regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles)
* 25d (237bhp) if buying V6 — the lower tune stresses the engine less
avoidIf: * 2016–2020 V6 with any cold-start knock or rattle — bearing risk is real
* Any evidence of oil pressure issues — critical on the 306DT
* Short-journey-only history — DPF issues are almost certain
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The F-Pace 25d/30d is a compelling luxury SUV, but the engine choice is critical. The 306DT V6 (2016–2020) is a powerful engine with a well-documented crankshaft bearing failure risk — avoid unless you have documented history. The AJ20D6 Ingenium I6 (2021+) is a significant improvement, smoother and more reliable. Buy the facelift, maintain the DPF/EGR system, and the F-Pace will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any knock or rattle (306DT bearing risk is the biggest warning)
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — pre-2021 = 306DT V6, 2021+ = AJ20D6 I6
* Oil Pressure — Any warnings? (306DT oil pump failure risk)
* Crankshaft History — 306DT: any bearing work done? Oil pump replaced?
* DPF History — Ask about driving patterns; frequent short trips cause DPF issues
* EGR System — Any coolant loss or white smoke? EGR cooler failure is common
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any whistling or lag? (306DT turbo issues)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying

items.question: How much does a Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Jaguar F-Pace 3.0 diesel engine costs £5,000–£8,000 supply only, varying by generation: 306DT £4,500–£7,500, AJ20D6 £5,500–£9,500. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

items.question: Is the Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d reliable?
items.answer: That depends on which engine your F-Pace has. The 306DT V6 (2016–2020) has a well-documented crankshaft bearing failure risk — this is the defining issue of the pre-facelift cars. The AJ20D6 Ingenium I6 (2021+) is a significant improvement, replacing the V6 with a smoother, more reliable inline-six. Buy the facelift if you can.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d?
items.answer: 306DT V6: crankshaft bearing failure (cold-start knock, £5,500–£7,500), oil pump failure (£1,500–£3,500), turbo failure (£1,500–£3,500), DPF/EGR issues (£400–£1,200). AJ20D6 I6: DPF/EGR issues (£400–£1,200), cooling system leaks (£400–£1,000).

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: F-Pace values range from £10,000–£45,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £5,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine represents 15–80% of vehicle value — our 60% Jaguar scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained facelift (AJ20D6), replacement is often justified. For an ageing pre-facelift 306DT with other issues, assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my Jaguar F-Pace 25d / 30d engine code?
items.answer: The F-Pace 25d/30d has used two engines: 306DT (2016–2020, 3.0L V6 diesel, 237/296bhp) and AJ20D6 (2021+, 3.0L I6 diesel, 300bhp). Check your VIN or registration year — pre-2021 is 306DT, 2021+ is AJ20D6. The code is stamped on the engine block.

items.question: Can I repair the 306DT crankshaft bearing issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: In many cases, engine replacement is the more cost-effective option (£4,500–£7,500) compared to a bottom-end rebuild (£5,500–£7,500). If caught extremely early — before bearing damage has scored the crankshaft — a specialist rebuild may be possible. But for most examples, replacement is recommended. At the first sign of cold-start knock, stop driving immediately and book an inspection.
